title: Olympiad-level formal mathematical reasoning with reinforcement learning
methods: AlphaZero-inspired tree search (PUCT, AND-OR nodes, progressive sampling)
supervised fine-tuning on Mathlib tactic pairs
distributed actor-learner RL with adaptive matchmaker curriculum
auto-formalization via fine-tuned Gemini 1.5 Pro with STaR-style iterative refinement
test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) with variant generation + evolutionary refinement
inference-time scaling via tree search budget and TTRL compute
data_sources: Auto-formalized problem dataset (~80M Lean problems from ~1M NL problems)
formal-imo benchmark (258 historical IMO problems in Lean)
Gemini 1.5 Pro (for auto-formalization and answer generation)

data_scope.pretraining_tokens: ~300 billion tokens of code and mathematical text
data_scope.sft_pairs: ~300,000 Lean tactic state-action pairs (~5M tactic tokens) from Mathlib
data_scope.rl_curriculum_problems: ~80 million formal Lean problems auto-formalized from ~1 million NL sources
data_scope.formal_imo_benchmark: 258 non-geometry IMO problems (107 algebra, 77 combinatorics, 74 number theory)
data_scope.imo_2024_problems_attempted: 5 non-geometry problems (P1-P3, P5-P6); P4 (geometry) by AlphaGeometry 2

core_conclusion: AlphaProof, an AlphaZero-inspired RL agent trained in the Lean formal proof assistant on a curriculum of ~80 million auto-formalized problems, achieves silver-medal performance at the 2024 International Mathematical Olympiad — the first AI system to reach any medal level at that competition. Test-time RL (TTRL), which generates millions of problem-specific variants at inference and runs focused RL on them, unlocks solutions to the hardest problems (including IMO 2024 P6, solved by only 5 of 609 human contestants) that extensive tree search alone cannot find. Formal verification by Lean's kernel guarantees correctness of every proof.
why_it_matters: First AI medal (silver) at the IMO — a qualitative leap over prior systems; formal verification closes the trust gap left by LLM-based solvers; TTRL introduces a new inference paradigm (problem-specific RL at inference time); auto-formalization pipeline makes formal math training scalable without slow human curation; new benchmark infrastructure (formal-imo, corrected miniF2F) raises the bar for the field.
why_nature: Clearly verifiable landmark milestone (first AI IMO medal); unambiguous reproducibility via formal kernel verification; broad societal significance (scientific discovery, software verification, education); TTRL as methodological innovation with cross-domain impact beyond mathematics.
added_value_to_my_research: TTRL variant-curriculum logic as inference-time adaptation for equity-constrained facility siting (EV charging, primary care)
Auto-formalization pipeline to translate spatial-planning regulations into machine-checkable Lean specifications for R2SFCA compliance verification
Two-axis inference scaling (search + TTRL) for spatiotemporal prediction: scenario-ensemble search + test-time fine-tuning on geographic variants of target region
Matchmaker 'interestingness' criterion as active-learning sampler for heterogeneous multi-source GeoAI training data
AND-OR tree search with min-return backpropagation for hierarchical spatial planning (tract → county → state accessibility sub-problems)

related.title: Efficient and scalable reinforcement learning for large-scale network control
related.relation: scalable RL agent design; distributed actor-learner framework comparable to AlphaProof's matchmaker-actor architecture

related.title: DeepSeek-R1 incentivizes reasoning in LLMs through reinforcement learning
related.relation: parallel work on RL for LLM reasoning; complements AlphaProof's formal approach with informal chain-of-thought RL; both published in Nature 2025

related.title: Spatial planning of urban communities via deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: applies deep RL to spatial planning — a concrete domain where TTRL's variant-curriculum idea could transfer

related.title: Loss of plasticity in deep continual learning
related.relation: continual learning dynamics; AlphaProof's matchmaker curriculum addresses similar catastrophic forgetting / plasticity trade-offs during long RL training

related.title: Algorithmic fairness and bias mitigation for clinical machine learning with deep reinforcement learning
related.relation: RL for fairness-constrained clinical decision-making; connects to AlphaProof's AND-OR fairness-aware spatial search ideas

figures.caption: AlphaProof core reasoning components: (a) Lean tactic environment state transitions, (b) proof network outputs (policy + value), (c) AND-OR tree search guided by the network.
AlphaProof learning and adaptation: (a) main RL loop (auto-formalize → AlphaZero RL → network update), (b) TTRL loop (variant generation → focused RL on target problem).
Learning progression during main RL: (a) prove/disprove/undecided rates on training curriculum; (b) held-out benchmark solve rates; (c) simulation efficiency improvement over training.
Inference-time scaling: (a) solve rates vs. tree search compute (TPU hours, log scale); (b) solve rates vs. TTRL compute (TPU days, linear scale).
AI system score of 28/42 at IMO 2024 relative to human competitors, placed within the silver-medal band (one point below gold threshold).
